title: Patterns — Shopify Polaris
insights: Polaris documents pattern compositions: Resource Index Layout, Resource Details Layout, App Settings Layout, Date Picking (list and range variants), Error Messages, Loading States, and Saving. Each pattern specifies the exact component set to compose (e.g., IndexFilter + IndexTable + Page + Card + Badge + ChoiceList for Resource Index), plus content guidance and interaction spec.

title: Resource Index Layout — Shopify Polaris
insights: Canonical pattern for list pages: uses IndexTable with bulk actions, IndexFilter for faceted filtering, Page component for header actions, Pagination component, and Badge for status. Specifies sticky header (awsui-h1-sticky variant), skeleton state on initial load, empty state when no items, and search-match highlighting. Anti-pattern: using Card-wrapped forms for list pages.

title: Resource Details Layout — Shopify Polaris
insights: Companion pattern to resource index. Specifies layout: Page (title + breadcrumb + primary action) + two-column InlineGrid (primary content 2/3 + metadata sidebar 1/3). Uses Card for content sections, ContextualSaveBar for unsaved-changes state, and Banner for page-level alerts. Distinguishes create vs edit mode behaviour.

title: App Settings Layout — Shopify Polaris
insights: Settings page pattern: BlockStack + Card + InlineGrid + Box. Sections separated into logical groups, each Card containing a SettingToggle or form fields. Contextual save bar appears only when there are unsaved changes. No side-nav tabs — single scrolling page for app settings with fewer than ~10 settings sections.

title: Date Picking (Date Range) — Shopify Polaris
insights: Date range selection recipe: Popover + Button trigger + InlineGrid (two-column: OptionList of presets + DatePicker calendar) + BlockStack + TextField for custom range entry. Presets like 'Last 7 days', 'Last 30 days', 'Custom range'. Popover closes on selection. Handles date formatting locale-aware.

title: Date Picking (Date List) — Shopify Polaris
insights: Single date selection from a list of presets via Popover + OptionList + Button. No calendar shown — suited for relative date selection (Today, Yesterday, Last 7 days). Lightweight pattern for dashboard filter scenarios.

title: Error Messages — Shopify Polaris (legacy)
insights: Error pattern taxonomy: inline field error (InlineError component under field), form-level error summary (Banner type=critical at page top), page-level error (full-page error state). Specifies that inline errors use red text + icon, error summary links to individual fields, and error messages must state the problem and the fix.

title: Patterns Overview — Atlassian Design System
insights: Atlassian patterns solve common user problems by composing components and foundations. 2025 update: AI/Rovo interaction patterns added as first-class patterns. Existing patterns cover: messages (inline, flags, banners), empty states, loading states, and AI chat interfaces. Patterns are separate from components — they prescribe which components combine and why.

title: Messages Pattern — Atlassian Design System
insights: Atlassian Messages pattern distinguishes: Inline message (contextual, inside content), Flag (toast notification — top-right, auto-dismiss for success, persistent for error), and Banner (full-width, page-level, above navigation for critical system messages). Specifies: success flags auto-dismiss in 5s, error flags require manual dismiss, banners are for outages/degradation only.

title: Patterns Overview — Carbon Design System (IBM)
insights: Carbon v11 pattern catalog: Dialogs, Empty states, Forms, Global header, Login, Notifications, Search, Filtering, Loading. Each pattern documents: overview, usage guidance, component composition, content guidance, accessibility, and live code. IBM definition: patterns are best-practice solutions for how a user achieves a goal — reusable combinations of components addressing common objectives.

title: Empty States Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon prescribes three empty state types: first-use (new user, no data yet), no-results (search/filter returned nothing), and error (data failed to load). Each has: illustration (optional, only if space permits), heading (mandatory), body text (max 3 lines), and CTA (optional action button). Anti-pattern: showing illustration in small tiles/toasts. Content guidance: solution-oriented message, not failure-focused.

title: Dialog Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon dialog pattern taxonomy: Transactional (requires user action to proceed — confirmation, destructive action), Passive (informational, no required action), Acknowledgment (user must confirm they read). Composition: Modal component + ModalHeader + ModalBody + ModalFooter. Destructive modals use danger variant button, cancel takes focus (not delete button). ARIA: aria-modal=true, focus trap.

title: Forms Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon forms pattern covers: simple (single column, single page), complex (multi-column, tabbed sections), wizard (multi-step with progress indicator). Layout rules: 2-column max on desktop, label above field (not inline), help text below field, error message replaces help text on validation fail. All required fields marked, not optional. Group related fields in fieldsets.

title: Filtering Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon filtering taxonomy: Single filter (one criterion at a time), Multi-filter batch (multiple at once, no auto-apply), Inline filter (within table column headers). Applied filters shown as tags that can be dismissed. Filter panel can be sidebar or inline depending on data complexity. Batch filtering: user sets all filters then clicks Apply — reduces unnecessary API calls.

title: Search Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon search pattern: global search (site-wide, prominent placement) vs local search (within page/component). Search component + Typeahead + recent-searches layer. Results page uses DataTable or structured list. Search is not filtering — filtering refines an existing set, search queries a backend. Scope indicator (e.g., 'Search all products') reduces ambiguity.

title: Notification Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon notification taxonomy: toast (temporary, top-right, 5-7s auto-dismiss), inline (persistent, inside content, requires manual dismiss), banner (full-width, page-level), action notification (includes a CTA link). Two styles per type: high-contrast (urgent/critical) and low-contrast (informational). Toast duration formula: 50ms x char count, min 2s, max 7s.

title: Loading Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon loading taxonomy: spinner (short indeterminate waits <10s), skeleton (page/section initial load — mimics final layout), inline loading (button/action-triggered), progress bar (determinate, file upload/batch). Skeleton appears on initial render before data arrives; skeleton nodes mirror exact final layout dimensions. Avoid stacking multiple loading spinners.

title: Design Guidelines — Salesforce Lightning Design System 2
insights: SLDS 2 renames 'guidelines' from SLDS 1 to 'patterns'. SLDS 2 separates structure from visual design via styling hooks (CSS custom properties). Patterns cover: data entry, data display, navigation, messaging, and loading. SLDS 2 Spring '25: new CSS framework, deeper customisation. Design guidelines include when-to-use, anti-patterns, and accessibility mandates.

title: Patterns — Salesforce Lightning Design System 2
insights: SLDS 2 pattern library covers: inline editing (table cells, form fields), read/edit state transitions, action menus (contextual row actions), related lists, activity timeline, dueling picklist, lookup (combobox with search), and rich text editor composition. Enterprise HR domain: candidate record pattern uses activity timeline + related list + inline edit composition.

title: Patterns — PatternFly (Red Hat)
insights: PatternFly patterns catalog: Bulk selection, Dashboard, Filters, Sticky header, Table with actions, Wizard, Empty state, Error state, Login page. Each pattern: usage guidance + component composition + accessibility. PatternFly is Red Hat's enterprise open-source system — patterns are reusable best-practice solutions containing multiple components. Distinct from components (atomic) and guidelines (principles).

title: Bulk Selection Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly bulk selection uses a SplitButton in the toolbar: left side selects/deselects all on page, dropdown offers 'Select all (N items)', 'Select page', 'Select none'. Selected count displayed in toolbar. Bulk action buttons appear in toolbar (not inline rows). Three selection scopes: page-level, global (all results), row-level. Checkbox in header = page scope only.

title: Filters Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly filter variants: text entry, single select, checkbox select, date range picker. Filter toolbar sits above data list/table. Applied filters shown as chip groups below toolbar — each chip shows attribute+value, has X to remove. 'Clear all filters' button resets all. FilterGroup (multiple filter types in one toolbar row). Empty state shown when all results filtered out.

title: Dashboard Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly dashboard card types: aggregate status card (count + icon + color-coded severity), trend card (sparkline + delta %), utilization chart (donut or bar), and events/activity card (timeline list). Grid layout: 4-column at full width, 2-column at medium. Cards never exceed 1/4 width unless a feature card (1/2). Dashboard = read-only overview, not an editing surface.

title: Inline Edit Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly inline edit: table row edit (pencil icon toggle in rightmost column, row enters edit mode, kebab replaced by check+close icons), form field edit (pencil icon next to field value, value becomes input in-place). Always show check (save) and X (cancel). Validation: inline error below field in edit mode. Anti-pattern: auto-save on blur without explicit confirmation in destructive contexts.

title: Wizard Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly wizard: vertical step nav on left (each step labelled, status: not-started, in-progress, complete, error), content area on right. Back/Next buttons in footer. Cancel link (not button). Progressive validation: validate current step before allowing Next. Optional steps: skippable, marked 'optional'. Review step at end shows all selections. Max 8 steps recommended.

title: Modal Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly modal sizes: sm (446px), md (default, 576px), lg (836px), xl (1176px). ModalHeader has close X button always. Destructive modal: danger Button for primary action, Cancel for secondary — but Cancel takes focus on open (not danger). No nesting modals. Wizard inside modal for complex multi-step confirmation flows.

title: Empty State — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly empty state: EmptyStateIcon (large, themed), Title (h1/h2 depending on context), EmptyStateBody (description), EmptyStateActions (primary button + secondary link). Size variants: xs (no icon), sm, lg, xl. Use xl for full-page empty state, sm/xs for empty states within cards or widgets. Content: 'No {noun} found' as heading + explain why + what to do.

title: Table View Pattern — AWS Cloudscape
insights: Cloudscape table view: full-page variant of Table component. Sticky header recommended (awsui-h1-sticky — title shrinks on scroll). Toolbar: search + filter + column selector + refresh. Bulk selection: page-scope by default, 'Select all N items' global scope. Split panel (60/40) optional for row-detail preview without navigation. Row actions via inline button or action menu dropdown.

title: Split View Pattern — AWS Cloudscape
insights: Cloudscape split view: table/card list (left 60%) + detail panel (right 40%). Side panel appears when row is selected. Avoids full-page navigation for quick detail inspection. Panel has SplitPanel component with close X. Anti-pattern: using split view when detail content requires deep editing (use full detail page instead). Best for read-heavy, comparison-heavy workflows.

title: Inline Edit Pattern — AWS Cloudscape
insights: Cloudscape inline edit: pencil icon on cell hover, click to enter edit mode (cell becomes FormField with input). Save via Enter key or checkmark icon, cancel via Escape or X. Validation shown inline below cell input. Only one cell edits at a time. Anti-pattern: using inline edit for complex multi-field relationships (use modal or detail page).

title: Table Multi-Select Pattern — HashiCorp Helios
insights: Helios defines three selection scopes: global (all results via SplitButton in toolbar), page-level (header checkbox), row-level (individual row checkbox). Selection persistence across pagination is documented as a complex design decision — default is page-scope only. Bulk actions appear in toolbar when selection > 0. Selected count badge displayed. Multi-select + filtering: specifies what happens to selection when filters change.

title: Form Patterns — HashiCorp Helios
insights: Helios form patterns: spacing follows 8px grid between sections. Form layout rules: label above input, helper text below input, error message replaces helper text. Groups of related fields use FormField wrapper. Multi-column forms (2-col max on desktop). Required fields marked with asterisk. Button placement: primary left, secondary right in button group.

title: Patterns — US Web Design System (USWDS)
insights: USWDS patterns go beyond components — they are documented solutions for complex tasks. Documented patterns: Complete a complex form (multi-step progress), Create a user profile, Enter an address, Request personal info, Connect communication preferences, Choose a language. Each pattern has research evidence, content guidance, and accessibility requirements. Federal government context: all patterns WCAG 2.1 AA compliant.

title: Progress Easily — USWDS Multi-Step Form Pattern
insights: USWDS multi-step form pattern: Step indicator shows current step, completed steps (checkmark), and total steps. Each step = one page. Navigation: Back button (preserve entered data), Continue button. Save and return later: store session data and issue a return URL. Error summary at page top + inline errors. Research finding: users complete complex forms 40% faster when progress is visible.

title: Validation Pattern — GOV.UK Design System
insights: GOV.UK validation: validate on submit, not on keystroke. Error summary at top of page (links to each field error). Error message inline next to each field. Messages worded same in summary and inline. Pattern: wait for submit before showing errors — avoids anxiety during typing. Error message format: '[Field name] must be [requirement]'. Field border turns red on error.

title: Addresses Pattern — GOV.UK Design System
insights: GOV.UK address pattern: address lookup (postcode lookup API + manual fallback) preferred over multiple free-text fields for UK addresses. Multiple text inputs only when country is known. Fields: address line 1, address line 2 (optional), town/city, postcode. Never combine city and postcode in one field. International addresses require different field set.

title: Spectrum — Adobe Design System
insights: Adobe Spectrum 2 (2025 release) organises system into: foundation, components, patterns, content, and voice/tone. Patterns are component sets solving common design problems. Example: login form pattern = TextField + PasswordField + Checkbox(remember me) + Button + Link(forgot password). Spectrum 2 brings adaptive color (light/dark/spectrum themes), density modes, and AI interaction patterns for Creative Cloud.

title: Material Design 3 — Google
insights: Material Design 3 (Material You) patterns: navigation patterns (NavigationBar bottom 3-5 items, NavigationDrawer side 5+ items, NavigationRail for desktop), search (SearchBar + SearchView composition), bottom sheets (modal and persistent variants), dialogs (basic, full-screen). M3 Expressive (2025): 35 new shapes, shape morphing, motion physics system. Dynamic color via Material Theme Builder.

title: Primer — GitHub Design System
insights: Primer (GitHub's design system) documents UI patterns for: filtering/search (in repositories, PRs, issues), empty states (Blankslate component — icon + heading + description + CTA), dialogs (Dialog component with focus trap), navigation (UnderlineNav, TreeView for file explorer, ActionMenu for overflow actions), and PageLayout (two/three column with sidebar). Octicons icon system integrated.

title: Design Systems 101 — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG defines component library vs pattern library: component library = atomic UI elements; pattern library = compositions of elements into reusable page sections or flows. A design system includes both + principles + documentation. Key research finding: teams with a pattern library ship 34% faster and have 28% fewer UI inconsistencies. Patterns encode decisions — they answer 'when' and 'how', not just 'what'.

title: Design Pattern Guidelines — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG research: a well-documented pattern contains: problem statement, solution, when to use (context), when NOT to use (anti-patterns), visual example, interaction spec, content guidance, accessibility notes, and code. Patterns without 'when not to use' documentation result in misuse. Pattern libraries should include: 5-7 core navigation patterns, 3-4 data display patterns, 2-3 feedback patterns.

title: Designing Empty States — Nielsen Norman Group (3 Guidelines)
insights: NNG 3 guidelines for empty states: (1) Explain why the space is empty and what it means for the user — not just 'Nothing here yet'. (2) Provide a constructive next step — primary CTA directly addresses the emptiness. (3) Match the visual design to the emotional context — first-use empty states can be celebratory, error empty states should be calm. Research: 68% of users abandon apps that show empty states without guidance.

title: Wizards: Definition and Design Recommendations — NNG
insights: NNG definition: wizards are best for long, unfamiliar tasks users complete rarely (setup, onboarding, compliance). NOT suited for tasks users do daily (use a form). Research: wizards reduce errors by 22% for complex tasks vs single-page forms. Guidelines: 3-10 steps (over 10 = break into multiple wizards), allow back navigation without data loss, show step count, validate per step not at end.

title: Bulk Actions: 3 Design Guidelines — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG 3 bulk action guidelines: (1) Show a contextual action bar when items are selected — don't hide bulk actions in a menu. (2) Show the count of selected items prominently. (3) For destructive bulk actions, always require confirmation. Floating contextual toolbar (appeared at screen bottom or above selection) outperforms static toolbar in usability tests.

title: Breadcrumbs: 11 Design Guidelines — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG breadcrumb research: location breadcrumbs (where you are) are most common and useful; path breadcrumbs (how you got here) are confusing. Place below global nav, above page title. Current page in breadcrumb should not be a link. Separator: > or / (not • or — which imply list). On mobile: show only parent page (single back link). Never use breadcrumbs as primary navigation.

title: Data Tables: Four Major User Tasks — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG research on data tables: 4 user tasks: look up specific value, compare values across rows, understand trends, filter to a subset. Design implications: sortable columns required for compare task; sticky first column for wide tables; row hover highlight for look-up task; clear pagination for large sets. Headers must be descriptive not abbreviated (users cannot decode 'Amt' vs 'Amount').

title: Skeleton Screens 101 — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG skeleton screen research: skeleton screens improve perceived performance by 20-30% vs spinners. Should mirror exact final layout (same widths, heights, position). Animate with shimmer (left-to-right gradient) not pulse (opacity). Use for initial page load — not for subsequent data updates (inline spinner better). Anti-pattern: skeleton that doesn't match final layout creates jarring reflow.

title: Accordions on Desktop — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG accordion research: on desktop, accordions slow users because content is hidden. Use when: users need overview of sections and will read selectively; page has dense information; content is reference (FAQ, settings) not task flow. Don't use when: users need to compare content across sections; sections are short; task requires sequential reading. Allow multiple sections open simultaneously on desktop.

title: Listboxes vs. Dropdown Lists — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG: use listbox (always-visible list) when ≤7 options and screen space allows. Use dropdown when >7 options or screen is constrained. Combobox (type-to-filter) when >15 options. Multi-select: use checkbox list or multi-select listbox — avoid native <select multiple> (poor affordance). Never use dropdown for binary yes/no (use radio buttons or toggle). Never for fewer than 3 options (radio buttons better).

title: Design Patterns Catalog — UI-Patterns.com
insights: UI-Patterns.com catalogs 80+ interface design patterns with problem/solution format. Key patterns documented: Autocomplete, Breadcrumbs, Calendar Picker, Cards, Continuous Scrolling, DataGrid, Dropdown, Forgiving Format, InplaceEditor (inline edit), LazyRegistration, Pagination, PayWall, ProgressiveDisclosure, Settings (dedicated pattern), TableFilter, Wizard. Each entry includes: problem, solution, examples, related patterns.

title: Inplace Editor Pattern — UI-Patterns.com
insights: UI-Patterns Inplace Editor: problem = navigating to edit page interrupts reading flow; solution = click to edit in situ. Three variants: text click-to-edit (underline/dotted border affordance), icon-triggered (pencil icon on hover), inline form (reveals full form row). Save options: auto-save on blur, explicit save button, Enter/Escape key. Anti-pattern: no visual affordance that text is editable.

title: Paywall Pattern — UI-Patterns.com
insights: UI-Patterns Paywall: problem = showing premium features to free users in a way that motivates upgrade without alienating them. Soft paywall: content partially visible, blurred/faded below fold + upgrade CTA. Hard paywall: feature completely inaccessible, locked icon + CTA. Best practice: frame as 'unlocking' not 'blocking'. Show feature value before paywall (demo/preview). Include social proof and risk reversal (free trial, money-back).

title: Settings Pattern — UI-Patterns.com
insights: UI-Patterns Settings: settings should be named 'Settings' (not Options/Preferences). Layout: side-nav tabs for complex apps (10+ sections), single scrolling page for simple apps. Group by: account, notifications, privacy, integrations, billing. Put destructive settings (delete account, revoke access) at the bottom, visually separated. Always show current state (toggle ON/OFF, current value).
